panels.description: Time since the controller pod started, derived from the oldest running container (container_start_time_seconds).
panels.title: Pod Uptime

panels.description: Current pod phase from kube_pod_status_phase.
panels.title: Pod Phase

panels.description: Cumulative container restart count over time. Each step up marks a restart event; flat means none. Drag to zoom into a window when investigating a specific restart.
panels.title: Container Restarts

panels.description: Number of times the controller pod was rescheduled to a different node in the last 24h. Detected via changes in kube_pod_info node label.
panels.title: Reschedules (24h)

panels.description: Number of server threads running inside slurmctld (slurm_controller_server_thread_count).
panels.title: slurmctld Server Threads

panels.description: Container filesystem read/write throughput (rate over 5m). Read positive, Write negative.
panels.title: Container Filesystem IO

panels.description: Inode usage of the controller-spool PVC. Slurmctld writes many small files, so the inode pool typically saturates before the byte capacity does — this gauge is the early-warning indicator for spool exhaustion.
panels.title: Spool PVC Inode Usage

panels.description: Bytes used vs total capacity of the controller-spool PVC over time.
panels.title: Spool PVC Used vs Capacity

panels.description: Used inodes on the controller-spool PVC over time, with total inode capacity as a dashed reference. A steady upward drift hints at an inode leak.
panels.title: Spool PVC Inodes Used

panels.description: Average per-IO wait time on the controller-host's block devices. High values indicate the underlying storage is the bottleneck (relevant for slurmctld spool writes).
panels.title: Disk Wait Time

panels.description: Open file descriptors on the controller node (system-wide). slurmctld can run out of FDs under heavy connection load. Max is omitted when the kernel reports it as effectively unlimited.
panels.title: File Descriptors

panels.description: Per-mountpoint read-only and device-error flags (1 = bad). All zero in healthy state — any non-zero series indicates a filesystem in trouble. Mountpoints that are read-only by design (e.g. /mnt/cloud-metadata) are excluded.
panels.title: Filesystem ReadOnly / Error

panels.description: Per-device read and write IOPS on the controller node.
panels.title: Disk IOps

panels.description: Per-device read/write throughput at the block-device layer. Differs from Container Filesystem IO (which is cgroup-scoped) — surfaces noisy-neighbour contention if any.
panels.title: Disk I/O Throughput

panels.description: Fraction of time each block device was busy serving I/O. Persistent values near 1 mean the device is the bottleneck.
panels.title: Disk I/O Utilization

panels.description: Total RPC call rate across all message types (rate of slurm_controller_rpc_calls_total over 5m).
panels.title: Overall RPC Rate

panels.description: Average RPC duration across all message types (sum of duration rate / sum of call rate, 5m window).
panels.title: Overall RPC Avg Latency

panels.description: RPC call rate broken down by message type (rate over 5m, stacked).
panels.title: RPC Calls per Message Type

panels.description: Average RPC latency per message type (rate(duration)/rate(calls), 5m window).
panels.title: RPC Latency per Message Type

panels.description: Top 10 users by RPC call rate (rate of slurm_controller_rpc_user_calls_total over 5m).
panels.title: Top Users by RPC Rate

panels.description: Top 10 users by aggregate RPC time consumed per second (rate of slurm_controller_rpc_user_duration_seconds_total over 5m). A value of 1 means a user kept slurmctld busy for one full thread-second every second on average.
panels.title: Top Users by RPC Time/sec

panels.description: Working-set memory of each container in the controller pod, stacked. The dashed line shows node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes — when the stacked total approaches it, the node is running out of allocatable memory.
panels.title: Memory Working Set

panels.description: Working-set memory as a percentage of the per-container memory limit. Containers with no limit set fall back to node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes (memory the kernel believes is allocatable without pressure) as the denominator.
panels.title: Memory Consumption %

panels.description: Active vs inactive memory on the controller node. Active is recently used; inactive is reclaimable on demand.

targets.legendFormat: Active - Memory that has been used more recently and usually not reclaimed unless absolutely necessary
Inactive - Memory which has been less recently used. It is more eligible to be reclaimed for other purposes

panels.title: Memory Active / Inactive

panels.description: Committed_AS vs CommitLimit. The kernel will refuse new allocations once Committed_AS approaches the limit (when overcommit is disabled).

matcher.options: CommitLimit - Amount of memory currently available to be allocated on the system

targets.legendFormat: Committed_AS - Amount of memory presently allocated on the system
CommitLimit - Amount of memory currently available to be allocated on the system

panels.title: Memory Committed

panels.description: Pages awaiting writeback to disk and dirty pages. Sustained high values can indicate slow storage or heavy IO.

targets.legendFormat: Writeback - Memory which is actively being written back to disk
WritebackTmp - Memory used by FUSE for temporary writeback buffers
Dirty - Memory which is waiting to get written back to the disk

panels.title: Memory Writeback and Dirty

panels.description: Minor and major page faults per second on the controller node. Major faults imply disk IO under memory pressure.

targets.legendFormat: Pgfault - Page major and minor fault operations

panels.title: Memory Page Faults

panels.description: Rate of OOM kill events on the controller node (node_vmstat_oom_kill).
panels.title: OOM Killer

panels.description: Rate of pages read from / written to disk by the kernel (node_vmstat_pgpgin/pgpgout). High values can correlate with memory pressure or heavy IO.
panels.title: Memory Pages In/Out

panels.description: Shared memory and file mappings on the controller node. slurmctld uses POSIX shared memory for IPC; large or growing values can hint at file-backed working set or IPC pressure.

targets.legendFormat: Mapped - Used memory in mapped pages files which have been mapped, such as libraries
Shmem - Used shared memory (shared between several processes, thus including RAM disks)
ShmemHugePages - Memory used by shared memory (shmem) and tmpfs allocated with huge pages
ShmemPmdMapped - Amount of shared (shmem/tmpfs) memory backed by huge pages

panels.title: Memory Shared and Mapped

panels.description: Anonymous (heap/stack) memory on the controller node. AnonPages is the closest single signal for slurmctld's actual memory footprint outside of file-backed caches.

targets.legendFormat: AnonHugePages - Memory in anonymous huge pages
AnonPages - Memory in user pages not backed by files

panels.title: Memory Anonymous

panels.description: Network throughput on the controller pod's network namespace (rate over 5m). RX positive, TX negative.
panels.title: Network Throughput

panels.description: Per-NIC packet rate (rate over 5m) on the controller node. Rx as positive, Tx as negative for visual symmetry.
panels.title: Network Traffic by Packets

panels.description: Per-NIC packet errors and drops on the controller node. Both should be zero in healthy state.
panels.title: Network Errors & Drops

panels.description: Currently established TCP connections on the controller node. For slurmctld this directly reflects how many slurmd/srun/sacct clients are connected.

targets.legendFormat: CurrEstab - TCP connections in ESTABLISHED or CLOSE-WAIT

panels.title: TCP Connections

panels.description: TCP-level errors and retransmits. Listen drops and retransmits are early signs of overload or network problems.

targets.legendFormat: ListenDrops - SYNs to LISTEN sockets ignored (overflow)
OutRsts - segments sent with RST flag

panels.title: TCP Errors & Retransmits

panels.description: Netfilter connection-tracking entries vs limit. slurmctld sees many concurrent connections; if entries approach the limit, new connections fail silently.
panels.title: NF Conntrack

panels.description: Per-NIC operational state. UP/carrier should be 1 in healthy state — drops to 0 indicate the interface is administratively down or the cable/link has gone away.
panels.title: Network Operational Status

panels.description: CPU cores consumed by each container in the controller pod (rate over 5m).
panels.title: CPU Usage (cores)

panels.description: Per-mode CPU utilisation on the controller node, stacked to 100%. Iowait, Steal, and Softirq are particularly informative when the controller feels slow.

targets.legendFormat: System - Processes executing in kernel mode
User - Normal processes executing in user mode
Nice - Niced processes in user mode
Iowait - Waiting for I/O to complete
Steal - Time stolen by the hypervisor
Idle - Waiting for something to happen

panels.title: CPU Mode Breakdown

panels.description: System load averages over 1, 5 and 15 minutes. Includes runnable + uninterruptible-IO tasks; values trending above the CPU count indicate scheduling backlog.
panels.title: System Load

panels.description: Context-switch and interrupt rate on the controller node. Spikes here often correlate with bursts of slurmctld RPC traffic.
panels.title: Context Switches & Interrupts

panels.description:
Multi-dimensional pressure on the controller node:
• Kubelet MemoryPressure (binary 0/100) — when kubelet flags the node memory-pressured, it may evict pods.
• Memory PSI some/full — kernel fraction of time tasks were stalled waiting for memory.
• CPU PSI some — fraction of time tasks were stalled waiting for CPU.
• I/O PSI some/full — fraction of time tasks were stalled on I/O.

PSI usually rises before the kubelet condition trips.

targets.legendFormat: Memory PSI some - % time at least one task stalled on memory
Memory PSI full - % time ALL tasks stalled on memory
CPU PSI some - % time at least one task stalled on CPU
I/O PSI some - % time at least one task stalled on I/O
I/O PSI full - % time ALL tasks stalled on I/O

panels.title: Pressure

list.description: Controller pod to inspect. Discovered from kube_pod_info; defaults to controller-0.
list.label: Controller Pod
list.name: pod

list.description: K8s node hosting the selected controller pod. Hidden; used for joining node-exporter metrics.
list.label: Node
list.name: node

list.description: node-exporter `instance` label for the controller node. Hidden; used to filter node_* metrics.
list.label: Instance
list.name: instance
